_comment: Owner-locked model routing 2026-06-26 (updated 2026-06-29: agentic-tool adapter). Orchestrator is ALWAYS Opus 4.8 low; it spawns per-task workers. Non-Anthropic (OpenCode Go / Grok Build / Antigravity) models IMPLEMENT/AUDIT to save Claude quota but are NOT trusted: every code-mutating workflow MUST end with a mandatory Opus 4.8 xhigh review (see mandatory_final_review). Sonnet is dropped from dispatch (kept only as an emergency manual fallback). THREE PARALLEL AGENTIC BACKENDS (scripts/agent_exec.py adapter): opencode (paid, dollar-capped, $12/5h), grok (Claude Code-style, --effort ladder, --allow/--deny permission rules), agy (Gemini/Claude/GPT-OSS via Antigravity CLI). Fallback chain per role: when primary backend quota-exhausted/timeout/auth-fail, agent_exec tries fallback[i+1]. Review roles (audit/audit_second_lens/final_review) set allow_fallback=false — glm-5.2 review is mandatory per owner, no fallback. TWO PARALLEL TIERS BY DESIGN: `roles`+`agentic_ladder` = ACTUAL worker dispatch (read by select_worker_routing); `tiers`+`escalation_ladder`+`coordinator`+`verifier` = Anthropic-equivalent SAFETY baseline (read by select_model_effort). Both agree on the safety floor. Full SSOT explanation: docs/harness/notes/tier-policy-ssot.md. Plan: docs/harness/plans/opencode-multimodel-integration-plan-2026-06-26.md

anthropic.note: Claude Code / Claude CLI native dispatch

opencode.note: OpenCode Go; PAID models only (no -free, rate-limit risk); single dollar-capped seat $12/5h $30/wk $60/mo

grok.note: Grok Build TUI; Claude Code-style flags (--effort, --allow/--deny, --agent); supports Claude + GPT-OSS + other models

agy.note: Antigravity CLI; Gemini 3.5 Flash / Gemini 3.1 Pro / Claude Sonnet 4.6 / Claude Opus 4.6 / GPT-OSS 120B; effort is model-fixed; --sandbox for restricted mode

backend_defaults._note: Default backend configs read by scripts/agent_exec.py when role doesn't override. `fallback_chain` = order to try when primary fails (quota/timeout/auth). Review roles set allow_fallback=false to force glm-5.2.

native.note: Claude Code Task tool — agent_exec refuses native roles; dispatch via Claude subagent instead

mandatory_final_review.rule: Every code-mutating workflow MUST end with an Opus 4.8 xhigh review of the produced diff, ALWAYS — even if a non-Anthropic model (glm-5.2/kimi-2.7/etc.) already reviewed. Non-Anthropic review is additive, NEVER a substitute. Rationale: cheap models are allowed to implement to spare Claude quota, but are not trusted; Opus does the final careful review.

tiers._baseline_note: Anthropic-equivalent SAFETY baseline only (read by select_model_effort). NOT the actual dispatch — see roles/agentic_ladder + select_worker_routing.

phases: ONE mimo-v2.5 worker (role=trivial) via oc_worker + harness gate — the PM still DISPATCHES; it never reads/edits source itself

single-skill.situation: T1 / small (few files, one component or one bug)

bugfix.situation: a BUG with a known repro (any tier)

triage-only.situation: verify a finding/bug list real-vs-false-positive, no fix

batch-bugfix.situation: a LIST of bugs to fix (batched by dependency phase)

design-only.situation: design only (distill + spec), stop before implement

full-SDD.situation: T3 OR module/feature-set OR a BA-doc/spec-file input
